mechanism_narrative: With FEDFUNDS near the zero lower bound, discount rates applied to equity cash flows remain compressed, sustaining elevated equity valuations and producing a persistent positive drift in S&P 500 forward returns as long as policy rates stay ultra-low.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.16% indicates near-zero policy rate
failure_condition: 20-day forward SPX returns are not significantly positive (t-test p>0.05) during periods when FEDFUNDS < 0.5%, or Rank IC falls below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window during such periods.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in UNRATE of more than 0.5 percentage points over a rolling 3-month window from a cyclical peak signals a genuine labor-market recovery, which raises expected corporate earnings and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent quarter.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE falls more than 0.5pp over trailing 3 months from a local peak
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 8.2% reflects elevated post-shock unemployment consistent with a recovery phase
failure_condition: 60-day forward SPX returns following triggered UNRATE declines are not higher than unconditional baseline returns, or the effect reverses sign in more than one out of three tested historical recoveries.

mechanism_narrative: When RSAFS and HOUST both rise simultaneously over a 2-month window, it indicates broad-based consumer and housing demand recovery that jointly signals sustained aggregate demand strength, which is not captured by either series alone and predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 404596 and HOUST at 717 reflect concurrent demand-side activity
regime_hint: any regime with synchronized consumer and housing momentum
failure_condition: Joint positive momentum in RSAFS and HOUST fails to outperform single-series models in out-of-sample Rank IC, or the interaction term contributes no incremental predictive power (delta IC < 0.01) versus the additive baseline.

mechanism_narrative: In a post-recession recovery regime, month-over-month acceleration in INDPRO reflects rebuilding industrial capacity and supply-chain normalization, which supports corporate earnings recovery and produces positive S&P 500 returns over a quarterly horizon; this relationship is expected to be weak or absent during late-cycle expansions where industrial output growth reflects capacity constraints rather than recovery.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 97.29 below the 2017=100 base level suggests below-trend industrial output consistent with recovery phase
failure_condition: INDPRO acceleration fails to predict positive 60-day forward returns in recovery-labeled periods (Rank IC < 0.03), or the sign of the relationship flips positive-to-negative when tested in late-cycle expansion subsamples without being flagged as regime-dependent.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL year-over-year inflation crosses above 3%, markets begin pricing in the risk of imminent monetary tightening, raising discount rates and compressing equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the following trading week.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 228.527 is the reference level for computing YoY inflation trigger
regime_hint: rising inflation regime with policy tightening risk
failure_condition: 5-day forward SPX returns following CPI YoY crossing above 3% are not significantly negative, or occur positive in more than 55% of triggered instances across historical episodes.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent month-over-month growth in PAYEMS reflects expanding aggregate labor income and consumer spending capacity, which sustains corporate revenue growth expectations and produces a steady positive drift in S&P 500 returns over the following month.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 133009 thousand persons provides baseline employment level for trend calculation
regime_hint: any regime with positive payroll trend
failure_condition: 20-day forward SPX returns show no significant positive correlation with trailing 3-month PAYEMS growth (Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window).

mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS is near zero and HOUST is simultaneously rising, cheap mortgage financing amplifies housing demand recovery beyond what either factor would predict alone, generating a compounding wealth-effect boost to consumer confidence and equity prices over a quarterly horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.16% and HOUST at 717 co-occur in current snapshot
regime_hint: zero lower bound combined with housing recovery
failure_condition: The interaction of low FEDFUNDS and rising HOUST fails to outperform either single-series model in out-of-sample Rank IC over 60-day horizon, or incremental IC from the interaction term is below 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: When UNRATE remains above 8% and shows no month-over-month improvement, it signals labor market stagnation that increases near-term downside risk to consumer spending and corporate earnings, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the following trading week.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE above 8% and unchanged or higher month-over-month
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 8.2% exceeds the 8% stagnation threshold
failure_condition: 5-day forward SPX returns following triggered UNRATE stagnation are not significantly negative, or positive in more than 55% of triggered historical instances.
